libraryPreparationPreflight.agent_guidance: Use Scala tests placed in package org.apache.pekko or org.apache.pekko.macros so package-private private[pekko] APIs are accessible. For 2.0.0-M1, do not import or test org.apache.pekko.http.ccompat.pre213/since213 classes; they existed in 1.3.0 but are no longer present. Focus coverage on org.apache.pekko.macros.LogHelper and LogHelperMacro, for example with a small in-package LogHelper implementation that invokes debug/info/warning without requiring external services.

deterministic_setup.reason: pekko-parsing declares pekko-actor as a provided dependency, and its LogHelper API directly references org.apache.pekko.event.LoggingAdapter and org.apache.pekko.annotation.InternalApi.
The artifact contains Scala 2 macro implementations using scala.reflect.macros.blackbox, and its POM declares scala-reflect 2.13.18 as provided.

input_evidence.name: issue
input_evidence.summary: #8878 [Automation] java run fails for org.apache.pekko:pekko-parsing_2.13:2.0.0-M1; label=fails-java-run; body_chars=8015

risks: The artifact has only internal/private Pekko macro APIs in 2.0.0-M1, so generated tests that assume public parser APIs or old ccompat annotation classes will fail to compile.
Scala macro tests are sensitive to Scala version alignment; local Gradle and Native Image verification remain authoritative.
libraryPreparationPreflight.summary: The 2.0.0-M1 artifact is a tiny internal Scala macro jar; meaningful coverage requires its provided dependencies on the test classpath and tests must target the remaining macro APIs rather than removed ccompat classes.
